title: Economy from 1945 to 1960
description: Test your skills on the era of economic prosperity!

assessment_items.question_content:
> “With the post-1945 emergence of America from the Great Depression and World War II, the automobile moved the nation forward into the modern age. The pioneering efforts of Henry Ford to mass-produce the automobile and offer affordable prices for average Americans created a new product called ‘personal travel’ and a host of new business opportunities. . . the construction of the vast interstate highway system was largely concentrated in the years 1958-72. The multi-lane highway network greatly increased the productivity of truck drivers, shortened the duration of personal trips, and increased safety.” 

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living Since the Civil War,* 2016

**The rise of highways, as described in the excerpt, most directly contributed to which of the following characteristics of United States society during the period?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the improvement to standards of living
the mass migration to the Midwest
the massive cut to federal spending
the advancement of public transportation

assessment_items.question_content:
> “With the post-1945 emergence of America from the Great Depression and World War II, the automobile moved the nation forward into the modern age. The pioneering efforts of Henry Ford to mass-produce the automobile and offer affordable prices for average Americans created a new product called ‘personal travel’ and a host of new business opportunities. . . the construction of the vast interstate highway system was largely concentrated in the years 1958-72. The multi-lane highway network greatly increased the productivity of truck drivers, shortened the duration of personal trips, and increased safety.” 

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living Since the Civil War,* 2016

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that increased federal spending had on the United States?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spurred economic growth
decreased international trade
lowered the price of goods
supported the car industry

assessment_items.question_content:
> “With the post-1945 emergence of America from the Great Depression and World War II, the automobile moved the nation forward into the modern age. The pioneering efforts of Henry Ford to mass-produce the automobile and offer affordable prices for average Americans created a new product called ‘personal travel’ and a host of new business opportunities. . . the construction of the vast interstate highway system was largely concentrated in the years 1958-72. The multi-lane highway network greatly increased the productivity of truck drivers, shortened the duration of personal trips, and increased safety.” 

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living Since the Civil War,* 2016

**The patterns described in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following developments?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the growth of service industries and tourism
the reduction in federal funding for the military
the realignment of the South to the Republican Party
the rise in birth rates for American families

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f7be8699d68518865f11c3f391485fc.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1946 to 1961 shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the economic prosperity felt after World War II
the elimination of birth control methods
the increased employment of women
the mass migration to the Northeast

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f7be8699d68518865f11c3f391485fc.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following factors contributed most to the major trend depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the end of World War II and the creation of the GI Bill
the start of the Korean War and the rise in Asian immigration
the start of Great Society programs and federal support for children’s healthcare
the end of the Great Depression and the focus on the agricultural economy

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f7be8699d68518865f11c3f391485fc.png)

>-Source: [US Census Bureau](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following historical developments was a direct effect of the trend illustrated by the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of suburban communities
the decrease in consumerism
the start of a civil rights movement
the emergence of a conservative movement

assessment_items.question_content:
> “If the G.I. Bill of Rights altered the landscape of higher education in America, it transformed the cultural and physical landscape of the country even more dramatically. The American suburban exodus of the 1950s did not simply spring into existence on its own: It is no exaggeration to say that the creation of suburbia and the resulting extension of home ownership to a majority of families in America was launched, underwritten, and paid for by the G.I. Bill.”

>-Source: Edward Humes, historian, *Over Here: How the G.I. Bill Transformed the American Dream,* 2014

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the 1950s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the improvement in social mobility for most Americans
the creation of government programs to support job training programs
the mass migration of Americans to cities in the Northeast and Midwest
the rise of a counterculture that criticized mass conformity

assessment_items.question_content:
> “If the G.I. Bill of Rights altered the landscape of higher education in America, it transformed the cultural and physical landscape of the country even more dramatically. The American suburban exodus of the 1950s did not simply spring into existence on its own: It is no exaggeration to say that the creation of suburbia and the resulting extension of home ownership to a majority of families in American was launched, underwritten, and paid for by the G.I. Bill.”

>-Source: Edward Humes, historian, *Over Here: How the G.I. Bill Transformed the American Dream,* 2014

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that the GI Bill had on American society?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It caused millions of American families to relocate to suburbs in the South and West.
It plunged the United States into a major recession after the federal deficit exploded.
It led to an increase in the number of men and women who enlisted in the military as active service members.
It generated a consumerist culture, in which American culture revolved around purchasing expensive goods.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “If the G.I. Bill of Rights altered the landscape of higher education in America, it transformed the cultural and physical landscape of the country even more dramatically. The American suburban exodus of the 1950s did not simply spring into existence on its own: It is no exaggeration to say that the creation of suburbia and the resulting extension of home ownership to a majority of families in American was launched, underwritten, and paid for by the G.I. Bill.”

>-Source: Edward Humes, historian, *Over Here: How the G.I. Bill Transformed the American Dream,* 2014

**Which of the following pieces of historical evidence from the United States census could best be used to support the argument in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: data showing the number of veterans who bought houses
data showing changes in the number of suburban neighborhoods
data showing the average earnings of American households
data showing population growth in the South and West

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Population by region, 1940-1960** ||
Region | 1940 | 1950 | 1960 | 1970 | Percent change
- | - | - |- | - | -
Northeast | 35,977,000 | 39,478,000 | 44,678,000 | 49,041,000 | 36\% 
North Central | 40,143,000 | 44,461,000 | 51,619,000 | 56,572,000 | 41\%
South | 41,666,000 | 47,197,000 | 54,973,000 | 62,795,000 | 51\%
West | 14,379,000 | 20,190,000 | 28,053,000 | 34,804,000 | 142\%

>-Source: [US census](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following best explains the overall population trend shown from 1940 to 1970?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People relocated to the Sunbelt at greater rates than any other region.
Immigration increased to all regions after recovery from the Great Depression.
Population in suburban regions increased faster than in rural or urban areas.
Refugees from war-torn countries settled in the West more than another region.

**The overall trend from 1940 to 1970 depicted in the table was most directly caused by which of the following factors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: creation of more economic opportunities
improvements in personal transportation
development of new manufacturing jobs in the North
migration of major religious groups to the West

**Which of the following statements best describes the long-term effect that the migration depicted in the table had on American politics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Sunbelt emerged as a significant political force, holding a majority of electoral votes.
The Northeast held the majority of seats in the House of Representatives.
The North Central states became key battleground states in presidential elections.
The West lost a significant amount of political capital as the population decreased.
